title: Today's Tech Brief: Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up
summary: A look at Anthropic's new Claude model and a critical Drupal patch.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got two tech headlines that caught my eye. First up, Anthropic's Claude has a new model that's more honest when it messes up. I am looking at the Verge's report on Opus 4.8, launching on Thursday. This new model seems like a breath of fresh air in the AI world.

According to the Verge, Anthropic's new model, Opus 4.8, aims to improve 'honesty' when it makes mistakes. My read is that this is a significant step in AI development, as it shows a growing focus on transparency and accountability. It's like when I mess up a recipe, I own up to it and tell my family it's an 'experimental dish'. Kidding aside, this could set a new standard for AI interactions.

Next, there's a critical patch for Drupal users. The Register reports that admins need to install a highly critical core patch. What stands out to me is that this patch affects unsupported 8.9 branches, which could leave many sites vulnerable if not updated. A quick action is required here to avoid any potential security issues.

Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: AI News Anthropic Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up Opus 4.8 is launching on Thursday. Opus 4.8 is launching on Thursday. by Jay Peters May 28, 2026, 5:00 PM UTC Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images Jay Peters is a senior reporter covering technology, gaming, and more. He joined The Verge in 2019 after nearly two years at Techm... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Clear your calendar, Drupal user: You have a critically urgent patch to install is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Security Clear your calendar, Drupal user: You have a critically urgent patch to install The org’s staying mum on the details, but Wednesday’s fixes reach back to unsupported 8.9 branches Brandon Vigliarolo Brandon Vigliarolo Published tue 19 May 2026 // 16:56 UTC Updated : If you use Drupal, get ready to patch without delay. The org behind the popular open... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
